## AI sales platform for modern B2B teams

## AI sales platform for modern B2B teams

Email marketing

Lead generation software

AI sales tools

The only GTM orchestration platform you will need to successfully take your products & services to market. Pebbles AI is a Go-To-Market Operating System built for B2B revenue teams. It brings strategy, lead generation, outreach, sales, & shared company knowledge into one AI-powered workspace. Using neurosymbolic AI trained on your business, it helps teams plan campaigns, personalize outreach, generate qualified leads, & execute without switching between disconnected GTM tools. [...] What is the solution?

Pebbles Ai brings your strategy, audience targeting, and outreach into a single, connected loop. It combines advanced neurosymbolic AI with real B2B growth expertise so teams can work together in one place.

 Learns your product and tone of voice from your brand docs, so you never re-explain your business
 Reads B2B market signals to find your ideal customers and split them into clear segments
 Acts like an in-house strategy consultancy and content agency in one
 Crafts multi-touch campaigns tailored to each segment automatically
 Keeps your data private in tenant-isolated architecture (CASA II certified) [...] Before you know it, your day is swallowed by a chaotic pile of disconnected AI tools. Teams end up wasting time switching between tabs, losing context, and burning budgets on a dozen different subscriptions just to align sales and marketing. It shouldn't feel this fragmented, and it definitely shouldn't feel this overwhelming.

We built Pebbles Ai to collapse that heavy, expensive stack into one secure, unified workspace for your entire growth team.

Who It's For?

B2B growth teams and professionals across sales, marketing, and RevOps trying to land their first or next customers.

What is the solution?

results.title: AI in B2B customer support is a slap in the face
results.content:
- Valid frustration, but I think the issue is down to poorly set up AI support rather than anything else. I'm the founder of an AI customer service business and one of our principles has always been that it has to be easy to speak to a person if someone needs to. We do this by giving multiple ways to escalate - ask to speak to a person, click a button to speak to someone, ask about specific topics AI can't handle, if we notice frustration or repetition, all of these will auto-escalate - it's the only way we can get a true reflection on the AI's performance and make it better. (and we also summarize the conversation on escalation automatically so you don't have to repeat yourself) I've had good and bad AI support experiences myself - Clay and Twilio were great examples (not using our [...] - The problem isn't even the AI part. It's that these companies are using bots as a cost cutting measure disguised as a feature. They don't want you to reach a human. The bot isn't broken, it's working exactly as intended. Deflect deflect deflect until the customer gives up or figures it out themselves. I run a couple businesses and we pay good money for B2B tools. The idea that I'm paying five figures a year for software and can't talk to a person when something breaks is insane. And you're right about the summarization thing, that's the part that really tells on them. They have the technology to make the handoff smooth and they just... don't. Because the whole point was never to help you faster. It was to make support cheaper for them. [...] - Go onto any App Store: Apple/Google And you will find real comments of real users complaining about this exact thing From major health care companies to big software brands They just don’t care at this point Which I do think creates a market need and gap for businesses that can fill this need and provide a service or product
- I run a B2B tech support team (later stage startup) and it boils my blood whenever I'm urged to check out one of these AI tools. They are completely incapable of providing the level of support that paying customers should expect yet they're touted as a full support replacement. For what we charge our customers, the least we can do is provide competent support.
- LLM’s are the ultimate B2B grift - an evolution even.
